journal_entry.description: Meal purchase on corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Meal expenses can map to different accounts depending on business purpose: Meals & Entertainment (50% deductible client meals), Travel Expenses (employee travel meals), Office Expenses (working meals during meetings), or Employee Benefits (staff meals). Each has different tax treatment and deductibility rules under CRA guidelines. Without knowing the business context, we cannot determine the correct account classification.
clarification_questions: What was the business purpose of this meal? (e.g., client entertainment, employee working meal, team meeting, travel meal, etc.)

output.reason: Meal expense = expense increase. The $125 meal is a business expense (meals and entertainment). The corporate credit card is the credit side (liability increase), not part of the debit classification.

debit_classifier.reason: Meal expense = expense increase. The $125 meal is a business expense (meals and entertainment). The corporate credit card is the credit side (liability increase), not part of the debit classification.

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase. When a purchase is made on credit card, the credit card payable (liability) increases.

credit_classifier.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase. When a purchase is made on credit card, the credit card payable (liability) increases.
